title: Groq Whisper Transcription in 50 Lines
hook: A 50-line Node.js service that transcribes a folder of audio files using Groq's Whisper-large-v3-turbo. Around 250x realtime, $0.04 per audio hour, drop-in replacement for OpenAI Whisper. Full code, format handling for m4a/mp3/wav/mp4, chunking strategy for files over 25MB, the three errors you will hit, and three real pipelines.
benefits: Why Groq Whisper beats OpenAI Whisper in 2026. Groq runs Whisper-large-v3-turbo at 250x realtime vs OpenAI's 5 to 10x. Pricing $0.04/audio hour vs OpenAI's $0.36. Quality parity on same model weights. Where OpenAI still wins: long-context streaming, built-in diarization.

The 50-line service in one file. Node.js, fs plus axios plus form-data, no exotic dependencies. The full code block, then a section-by-section walkthrough so you understand every line. Reads a folder, loops files, posts to Groq, writes the transcript to disk. That is the whole shape.

End-to-end setup in 5 minutes. Get a Groq API key from console.groq.com. Run npm install. Drop a placeholder .env. Point the script at your audio folder. First transcript hits disk before your coffee finishes brewing. The exact commands and the exact .env template.

Format handling for the four formats you encounter. m4a, mp3, wav go straight to the API. mp4 needs a one-line ffmpeg pre-step. Groq's 25MB ceiling and the chunking strategy. Ffmpeg commands to split a 90-min interview into 15-min chunks and stitch transcripts with offset timestamps.

The three errors you will hit and how to fix them. The 429 rate limit (5-line backoff wrapper). The file-too-large error (chunking pattern from Part 4). Garbage transcription from low-quality recordings (the ffmpeg preprocess command that fixes most bad-audio cases).

Three real pipelines you can ship this week. Podcast episode: 60-minute episode in 90 seconds with timestamps, ready for show notes. Meeting recording to action items: transcribe + downstream LLM call. Course video to searchable transcript library: chapter-tagged + indexed for student search.
squeezeBullets: 50-line Node.js service, the full code, format handling for m4a/mp3/wav/mp4, chunking for files over 25MB, three error fixes
Groq runs Whisper-large-v3-turbo at 250x realtime for $0.04 per audio hour, roughly 9x cheaper than OpenAI Whisper
Three working pipelines: podcast episode in 90 seconds, meeting to action items, course video to searchable transcripts
description:
If you are transcribing audio at any volume in 2026, you should not be paying OpenAI prices and waiting on OpenAI throughput. Groq runs the same Whisper-large-v3-turbo model weights at roughly 250x realtime against OpenAI's 5 to 10x, and the price is $0.04 per audio hour against OpenAI's $0.36. A 60-minute podcast episode finishes transcribing in about 90 seconds and costs four cents.

This playbook is the 50-line Node.js service I use across every audio job that crosses my desk. Six parts, dev-to-dev, no theory padding. Built for indie hackers shipping audio features, podcast operators producing show notes at volume, course creators turning video libraries into searchable transcripts, agencies running call-recording pipelines for clients, and anyone who has ever stared at a folder of m4a files and wondered if there was a faster way.

Part 1, Why Groq Whisper Beats OpenAI Whisper in 2026. The speed gap (250x vs 5 to 10x realtime) and what it means in practice (a 60-minute file in 90 seconds vs 6 to 12 minutes). The pricing gap ($0.04 per audio hour vs $0.36, roughly 9x cheaper). Quality parity on identical model weights, validated across English and Hindi audio in production. The two cases where OpenAI still wins (managed long-context streaming, built-in diarization that Groq does not yet ship).

Part 2, The 50-Line Service. The actual code in one block, then walked through section by section. The folder reader, the file loop, the axios POST to api.groq.com/openai/v1/audio/transcriptions, the disk writer for transcripts. No frameworks, no orchestration layer, no queue. One file, one Node process, run it from the terminal.

Part 3, Setup in 5 Minutes. Get a Groq API key from console.groq.com (free tier covers most personal projects). The npm install line. The .env template with the placeholder GROQ_API_KEY=your_groq_key_here so nothing real ships in the example. The exact terminal command to run your first transcription. What success looks like in the console output.

Part 4, Format Handling. m4a, mp3, wav go straight to the Groq endpoint with no pre-processing. mp4 needs a one-line ffmpeg pre-step to extract the audio track because Groq Whisper does not accept video containers. The 25MB file size ceiling and what it means for long audio. The chunking pattern: ffmpeg splits a 90-minute interview into six 15-minute chunks, the script transcribes each in parallel, and the chunk-stitcher offsets timestamps so the final transcript reads as one continuous file.

Part 5, The Three Common Errors and Fixes. Error 1: HTTP 429 rate limit. Fix is the exponential backoff wrapper, 5 lines. Error 2: file too large (the 25MB ceiling). Fix is the chunking pattern from Part 4. Error 3: garbage transcription from a low-quality recording (background noise, low bitrate, distant mic). Fix is the ffmpeg preprocessing command that normalizes audio gain and converts to 16kHz mono, which fixes most bad-audio cases.

Part 6, Three Real Pipelines. Pipeline 1: Podcast episode transcription. A 60-minute episode finishes in 90 seconds with timestamped segments. Stitched directly into the show notes workflow. Pipeline 2: Meeting recording to action items. Transcribe, then pipe the full text to a downstream LLM call (Claude or GPT) with a prompt that pulls out action items, decisions, and open questions. End deliverable in under 3 minutes per hour-long meeting. Pipeline 3: Course video to searchable transcript library. Each lesson transcribed, chapter-tagged, indexed in a Postgres full-text search column. Students search for a phrase and jump to the timestamp.

Included as a printable PDF and a duplicatable Notion reference. The GitHub repo at github.com/AI-BrandFactory/groq-whisper-transcription ships the working service plus the .env.example template. Built to be cloned on a Tuesday morning and running in production by Friday.
emailSubject: Your Groq Whisper Transcription playbook is ready
